key_insights.insight: Sports science research has historically been based on male data, generalizing findings to women which can be detrimental.
key_insights.quote: Everything from training to eating to recovery is based on male data. And women have been generalized to that data.

key_insights.insight: Women doing fasted training may burn lean mass because their hypothalamus is more sensitive to nutrient density compared to men.
key_insights.quote: All you've effectively done is burn through your lean mass...It would be better for you as a woman to have maybe 15 grams of protein...before you go do cardio and strength.

key_insights.insight: Women should eat 35 grams of protein within 45 minutes post-exercise to maximize muscle protein synthesis.
key_insights.quote: For women who are in their reproductive years, we see 35 grams of protein postexercise within 45 minutes. We'll tip the muscle into muscle protein synthesis.

key_insights.insight: Women need a cold plunge temperature of 15-16 degrees Celsius (55 degrees Fahrenheit) to experience the same benefits as men at lower temperatures.
key_insights.quote: We see that 15 to 16 degrees C, around 55 degrees Fahrenheit, is optimal temperature for women to experience the same effect that men have with ice.

key_insights.insight: For women, a typical menstrual cycle is more often 21-40 days, not the textbook 28 days.
key_insights.quote: Most women have a cycle that might be 21 to 40 days.

key_insights.insight: In perimenopause, women should focus on short, high-intensity cardio and power-based resistance training for less volume and more quality.
key_insights.quote: We're looking at doing short, sharp, high intensity cardio, or we're looking at doing power-based resistance training three times a week, and the cardio can be two to four times a week.

key_insights.insight: Women tend to suffer more with jet lag when travelling West because they're circadian rhythms are shorter than men.
key_insights.quote: For women going west, it's a little bit harder because it's longer...Women go better east than going west.

key_insights.insight: During the menstrual cycle, women can push for PRs in the low hormone phase and focus more on endurance or tempo type work after ovulation.
key_insights.quote: Where you know you can hit your PR and you can really push it in the low hormone phase. After ovulation, you're going to switch it to more endurance, maybe not so high intensity but more tempo type work.

actionable_advice.advice: Women should consider eating 15 grams of protein with 30 grams of carbohydrates before morning cardio or strength training.
actionable_advice.how_to: Consume a small snack of protein and carbohydrates, such as a protein bar or a piece of fruit with nut butter, before morning workouts.

actionable_advice.advice: Women should aim for 3-5 grams of creatine once a day, without carbohydrates, for gut and brain health.
actionable_advice.how_to: Add 3-5 grams of creatine to your water or smoothie each day, separate from workouts.

actionable_advice.advice: Women should aim for 30 different plants across the week to increase diversity of gut microbiome.
actionable_advice.how_to: Vary fruits, vegetables, nuts, and seeds in each meal, aiming for new additions every week.

actionable_advice.advice: Women who are in their reproductive years should eat at least 35 grams of protein post exercise within 45 minutes.
actionable_advice.how_to: Have a protein shake, protein bar, hard boiled eggs or a high protein meal within that timeframe.

actionable_advice.advice: Track your 'bleed pattern' and the length of your cycle to identify stress overload.
actionable_advice.how_to: Use a period tracking app or a journal to note the length of your cycle and characteristics of your period to understand what's normal.

memorable_stories: Stacey's experience in a metabolism lab where her data was discarded because it didn't align with the male data.
The story of young girl excited about getting into exercise physiology who hadn't been informed on the different between male/female physiology and muscle morphology.
A PhD student knew more about his partner's cycle than she did.
contrarian_claims: Women going through perimenopause and menopause do not need hormone replacement therapy, but menopausal hormone therapy, a lower does to attenuate severe change.
Overnight fasting is okay for women if they start eating within a half an hour of waking up to blunt cortisol and for men, they can have variations of fasting
Women with endrometriosis should use cold therapy (cold plunges) during ovulation to help mitigate and reduce growth of endometrial tissue.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Exercise & Nutrition Scientist: The Truth About Exercising On Your Period! Women Were Right About Menopause! These 4 Supplements Give Women Optimal Health!
